action.reasoning: Observation: predict/load contract is now aligned enough to produce valid outputs, but local val grounding_score is only 0.0032 (1/312), so the simple global pooled regressor is badly underfitting GUI grounding. Analysis: a single pooled vector cannot localize fine UI elements. We need a spatial model that outputs a heatmap conditioned on text, while keeping training fast. A lightweight FiLM-conditioned fully convolutional network over a 320x320 image can predict a click point heatmap and derive bbox around the point; this should substantially improve point accuracy with modest compute. Decision: replace the current pooled regressor with a spatial heatmap model using frozen-small text encoder (embedding+GRU), train for ~8 epochs, then run local val prediction/score and submit eval immediately if nontrivial.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 8 --batch_size 48 --lr 0.002 && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/simple_model.pt --output val_pred_heatmap.json && python score_val.py val_pred_heatmap.json
